_comment: Cross-archive concordance overlay. Keyed by record permalink key (caV-dN-nSCAN). Maintained by hand from vault projects/ca-archives/ca-cross-archive-concordance.md. kind: original|copy|counterpart|published.

ca12-d465-n391.text: The Mexico-City end of this affair survives as an original: Boris Tarasov's asylum petition, AGN (Mexico City), Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57 — enclosing Gov. Solá's 10 Nov 1815 dispatch. The Californian and viceregal sides of the same capture. Cf. Mathes, La frontera ruso-mexicana (1990).

ca12-d466-n391.text: The Mexico-City end of this affair survives as an original: Boris Tarasov's asylum petition, AGN (Mexico City), Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57. Cf. Mathes (1990).

ca13-d273-n216.text: Counterpart original in Mexico City: AGN, Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57 (Tarasov's asylum petition and the bureaucratic file San Blas→Mexico→Cádiz). This Savage copy is the sole survivor of the California-side inventory; the originals burned in 1906.

ca13-d274-n217.text: Counterpart original in Mexico City: AGN, Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57 (Tarasov asylum file).

ca17-d173-n114.text: The captured hunter of this report, Boris Tarasov, generated a counterpart file in Mexico City: AGN, Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57 (his asylum petition, ending 'se fugó para Panamá'). Cf. Mathes (1990).

ca17-d174-n115.text: Counterpart file in Mexico City: AGN, Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57 (Tarasov asylum petition).

ca17-d192-n153.text: The interrogated prisoner's Mexico-City file survives: AGN, Provincias Internas, vol. 23, exp. 4, ff. 44–57 (Tarasov asylum petition; encloses Solá's 10 Nov 1815 dispatch).
